scope.rules_of_engagement: Authorized penetration test. Full active recon permitted.

tools_used: nuclei v3.7.1 (re-run with corrected URL format — 1 match)

whois.raw: Target is a private IP address (10.3.10.10). WHOIS not applicable.

ports.banner: OWASP Juice Shop v19.1.1 running on Express ^4.21.0

web_technologies.cdn: Cloudflare CDN (cdnjs.cloudflare.com for cookieconsent 3.1.0 and jQuery 2.2.4)

issues: Target serves HTTP only — no TLS/HTTPS configured on port 1234

admin_panels: /api-docs/swagger.json (Swagger UI for NextGen B2B API v2.0, unauthenticated, 200)
file_exposures: /ftp/package.json.bak%2500.md — dependency manifest via null-byte bypass (reveals old dep versions)
/ftp/package-lock.json.bak%2500.md — full lock file via null-byte bypass
/encryptionkeys/jwt.pub — RSA public key used for JWT verification
/support/logs/audit.json — audit metadata with file paths and MD5 hashes

hardcoded_secrets: Google OAuth Client ID exposed in /rest/admin/application-configuration: 1005568560502-6hm16lef8oh46hr2d98vf2ohlnj4nfhq.apps.googleusercontent.com
JWT RSA public key at /encryptionkeys/jwt.pub (enables RS256→HS256 algorithm confusion attack)
websocket_endpoints: /socket.io/ (400 on plain HTTP GET, expects WebSocket upgrade)
interesting_comments: Copyright 2014-2026 Bjoern Kimminich & OWASP Juice Shop contributors — confirms OWASP Juice Shop intentionally vulnerable app

B2B API description: 'New & secure JSON-based API for our enterprise customers. (Deprecates previously offered XML-based endpoints)' — parenthetical strongly implies legacy XML endpoint still exists and may be vulnerable to XXE

security_headers.cors: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * (wildcard CORS — all origins allowed); Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET,HEAD,PUT,PATCH,POST,DELETE (all HTTP methods exposed)

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: SQLite SQL injection confirmed on product search endpoint. Single quote causes SQLITE_ERROR response leak. Backslash-escaped quote and boolean injection (`' OR 1=1--`) produce different behavior indicating injectable parameter.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /rest/products/search?q=\' → HTTP 500 with body: SQLITE_ERROR: unrecognized token

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: KeePass password database (incident-support.kdbx) is directly downloadable without authentication. May contain credentials for internal systems.

The /ftp file server restricts downloads to .md and .pdf files. This restriction is bypassable via double URL-encoded null byte (%2500) appended before a .md extension, allowing arbitrary file downloads including .bak, .pyc, .kdbx, and .gg files.

RSA public key for JWT verification is exposed at /encryptionkeys/jwt.pub. If the server accepts HS256-signed tokens, an attacker can use the public key as the HMAC secret to forge valid admin JWTs (RS256→HS256 algorithm confusion).
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /encryptionkeys/jwt.pub → 200, returns PEM-encoded RSA public key (1024-bit)

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Admin configuration and version endpoints are accessible without authentication, exposing server port, base URL, Google OAuth client ID, chatbot training data path, and full application configuration.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /rest/admin/application-configuration → 200, full JSON config including googleOauth.clientId and server.baseUrl

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Live HTTP access logs are publicly accessible, revealing all request patterns, user-agent strings, and IP addresses of users. Also includes audit.json with file paths and MD5 hashes.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /support/logs/access.log.2026-03-09 → 200, full Apache-format access log | GET /support/logs/audit.json → 200 with absolute server paths

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Premium feature key exposed at /encryptionkeys/premium.key. Combined with the JWT public key exposure, attackers have full cryptographic material to forge tokens and unlock premium features.
Prometheus metrics endpoint is publicly accessible, exposing process memory usage, CPU time, file descriptor counts, event loop lag, Node.js version details, and startup task durations.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /metrics → 200, full Prometheus text exposition format with process_* and nodejs_* metrics

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Multiple directories have directory listing enabled, exposing full file inventories including confidential documents, encryption keys, backup files, and logs.
Confidential business documents accessible without authentication: acquisitions.md (planned M&A details marked 'Do not distribute!'), legal.md (terms and legal info), coupons backup.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /ftp/acquisitions.md → 200 with text '> This document is confidential! Do not distribute!'

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Multiple outdated/vulnerable dependencies identified in package.json.bak: express-jwt 0.1.3 (multiple known CVEs), sanitize-html 1.4.2 (XSS bypass vulnerabilities), jQuery 2.2.4 (served from CDN, multiple XSS CVEs), sequelize ~4 (SQLi vulnerabilities), libxmljs ~0.18 (XXE vulnerability).
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: /ftp/package.json.bak%2500.md reveals full dependency manifest with pinned old versions

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Full server filesystem path disclosed in error responses: /opt/juice-shop/build/routes/fileServer.js. Also exposed in audit.json: /opt/juice-shop/logs/access.log.2026-03-09.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: HTTP 403 error on /ftp/*.bak includes full stacktrace with paths; audit.json contains absolute path

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Wildcard CORS policy (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) allows any origin to make cross-site requests. Combined with unauthenticated API endpoints, this enables cross-site data exfiltration.
Multiple important security headers are absent: Content-Security-Policy (no XSS mitigation), Strict-Transport-Security (no HTTPS enforcement), X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: curl -sI http://10.3.10.10:1234/ — no CSP, HSTS, or X-XSS-Protection headers present

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Full REST API and CRUD endpoints enumerable without authentication from Angular bundle (main.js). Endpoints include user management, order history, wallet, complaints, recycling, and Web3/NFT features.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: Extracted 40+ API routes from /main.js JavaScript bundle

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Application runs over HTTP only with no HTTPS. All data including credentials is transmitted in cleartext. SSH (port 22) is open on the same host.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: nmap top-1000 scan: port 22/tcp open OpenSSH 10.0p2 Debian 7, port 1234/tcp open http (Node.js/Express)

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Swagger UI is publicly accessible at /api-docs/swagger.json, exposing the full NextGen B2B API v2.0 specification without authentication. The spec documents a POST /b2b/v2/orders endpoint. The API description notes it 'deprecates previously offered XML-based endpoints', implying a legacy XML endpoint may still be active and potentially vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) injection.
potential_vulnerabilities.evidence: GET /api-docs/swagger.json → HTTP 200, Swagger UI HTML. GET /api-docs/swagger-ui-init.js → full OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec inline: title='NextGen B2B API', paths={'/orders': ['post']}, servers=[{url: '/b2b/v2'}]

potential_vulnerabilities.detail: Nuclei v3.7.1 confirmed Swagger API documentation endpoint. Previous nuclei run was skipped entirely due to an IP:port URL parsing bug (target was misinterpreted as IPv6 address). Re-run with corrected URL format returned 1 match.

osint.notes: Target is a private IP (RFC 1918 range 10.x.x.x). OSINT techniques (crt.sh, WHOIS, Wayback Machine, Google dorks, subfinder) are not applicable. Application is OWASP Juice Shop v19.1.1 — an intentionally vulnerable training application. All 'vulnerabilities' are by design for penetration testing practice.

high_value_targets: /encryptionkeys/jwt.pub — RSA public key for JWT forge (CRITICAL)
/b2b/v2/orders — B2B orders API, likely accepts XML (XXE candidate, MEDIUM)
/api-docs/swagger.json — exposed Swagger UI with full B2B API spec (MEDIUM)
/api/Users/ — user enumeration (requires auth bypass to access)
recommended_next_steps: EXPLOIT: SQLite SQL injection on /rest/products/search?q= — attempt UNION-based data extraction to dump the Users table (emails, password hashes)
EXPLOIT: JWT algorithm confusion attack — use exposed RSA public key from /encryptionkeys/jwt.pub as HS256 HMAC secret to forge admin JWT token
EXPLOIT: Download and crack /ftp/incident-support.kdbx KeePass database using hashcat/john — may contain SSH or admin credentials
EXPLOIT: Null-byte filter bypass on /ftp — download package.json.bak and package-lock.json.bak to identify specific CVEs in pinned dependency versions

EXPLOIT: Test XXE on POST /b2b/v2/orders — API description explicitly says it replaces 'XML-based endpoints', strongly suggesting XML input is still accepted or parsed; send Content-Type: application/xml with DOCTYPE/ENTITY payload
EXPLOIT: Use premium.key (1337133713371337.EA99A61D92D2955B1E9285B55BF2AD42) to unlock premium/deluxe membership at /rest/deluxe-membership
EXPLOIT: XSS testing on feedback/product review endpoints using CDN-loaded jQuery 2.2.4 (multiple stored XSS vectors known in Juice Shop)
EXPLOIT: Attempt authentication bypass on /api/Users/ — likely requires JWT token; use forged admin JWT from algorithm confusion
EXPLOIT: Probe /rest/user/reset-password and /rest/user/security-question for account takeover via predictable security question answers (14 questions enumerated)
EXPLOIT: Attempt to access SSH (port 22) using any credentials recovered from KeePass database or SQL injection
